items.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers have WA quarantine period extended

children.content: Six people who were on a cruise ship that was hit by an outbreak of hantavirus have had their quarantine period in the outer Perth suburb of Bullsbrook extended until June 23.

gallery.altText: The exterior of a quarantine facility on the outskirts of Perth.

thumbnail.altText: The exterior of a quarantine facility on the outskirts of Perth.

avatar.alt: A young woman named Lauren Smith wears a light coloured blazer over a black shirt.

items.title: Dry winter could make spring bushfire conditions in WA and NSW 'tricky'

children.content: A national bushfire outlook forecasts above-average daytime and overnight temperatures for winter and an increased risk for WA and NSW.

image.altText: Generic images of flames, equipment and RFS volunteers and staff conducting a hazard reduction burn at Tura Beach, NSW

items.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate in coming decades

children.content: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.

image.altText: The third will collected by the Office of the Public Trustee Queensland.

items.title: Mother whose two children died suddenly calls for genetic testing

children.content: A Perth woman who lost two children to an extremely rare medical condition calls for mandatory genetic testing for children whose cause of death is uncertain.

gallery.altText: A mother with blonde hair and a white dress stands in front of the ocean holding a young baby girl in a white outfit.
A young baby boy dressed in a red and green Christmas elf outfit sits on a couch smiling and laughing.
A smiling baby girl sits on the beach with a bucket wearing a white and green outfit and hat.

thumbnail.altText: A mother with blonde hair and a white dress stands in front of the ocean holding a young baby girl in a white outfit.
A young baby boy dressed in a red and green Christmas elf outfit sits on a couch smiling and laughing.
A smiling baby girl sits on the beach with a bucket wearing a white and green outfit and hat.

items.title: No further report on missing South West swimmer as police scale back search

children.content: Water police
have wound down the search
for a
swimmer who was reported to be in distress at Koombana Bay in Bunbury yesterday.

image.altText: A white ute with writing on it and surfboards on top of tray

items.title: Students help raise $100,000 for hay runs amid 'heartbreaking' drought

children.content: Emergency hay deliveries to drought-stricken areas resume after thousands of dollars in donations from school students and charity groups.

image.altText: a machine is loading hay bales

items.title: WA leading the charge as batteries disrupt global energy

children.content: As the use of big batteries booms across the world, Western Australia in particular is proving that grid-scale installations are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

gallery.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

thumbnail.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset
A man in high-vis wearing a hard hat stands before rows of white shipping-container-sized batteries

items.title: Australia secures first vessel for strategic fleet

children.content: Australia has secured the first vessel for its maritime strategic fleet, which is to be comprised of up to 12 privately owned, commercially operated ships. Follow live.

items.title: AFP defends public arrest of Ben Roberts-Smith

children.content: The Australian Federal Police has detailed intelligence surrounding Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest, pushing back against criticisms of its public nature.

image.altText: A blonde woman in a navy police officer uniform.

items.title: French Open favourite loses to 56th-ranked amid dizzy spell

children.content: Jannik Sinner's French Open campaign is over after the world number one struggled with illness in a shock second-round defeat to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

image.altText: Jannik Sinner sits in discomfort during a change of ends at the French Open.

items.title: Labor campaigned on 'integrity' so the NACC should be enjoying public trust by now

children.content: In an extraordinary irony, the National Anti-Corruption Commission has become best known for "own goals", rocked by internal scandals and attracting a litany of complaints.

image.altText: An older man in a suit with glasses leans in as he speaks into a microphone.

items.title: Specific needs 'soar' across school system over past decade, principal says

children.content: WA teachers will be taught how to better support children with higher needs amid rising rates of disability diagnoses, after a report found public schools needed to do more.

gallery.altText: Students are having a math class.
Two girls are having a class in a classroom.
A principal smiles to his students in a classroom.

thumbnail.altText: Students are having a math class.
Two girls are having a class in a classroom.
A principal smiles to his students in a classroom.

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of jobs in search for white collar workers

children.content: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly-skilled white collar workers overseas.

image.altText: A large outdoor Officeworks sign in front of large trees.

items.title: What does it mean to be the best jack of all trades, but master of none?

children.content: Elite athletes are constantly striving for perfection — an extra millisecond shaved off their time or another centimetre on their distance — but for heptathletes like Tori West, the equation is a little bit harder.

image.altText: An image of Tori West running surrounding by six pictures of her in heptathlon events.

items.title: Refinery on WA's south coast says it could replace region's diesel with biofuel

children.content: In the 1990s, Esperance was seen as a key to a major biofuels industry for Western Australia. Thirty years later, the industry has seen little change. But some say the area has untapped potential as a major fuel supplier.

gallery.altText: A field of flowering yellow canola.
A truck on a pile of wood chips at a refinery

thumbnail.altText: A field of flowering yellow canola.
A truck on a pile of wood chips at a refinery

items.title: 20 million illegal vapes seized by border force in just over two years

children.content: More than 20 million illegal vapes, with a street value of just over $1 billion, have been seized by law enforcement authorities since Australia's vaping reforms came into effect.

image.altText: Vape products in a gloved hand.

items.title: Friday news quiz: A drone drama, a 'dream job' and an old insult making a comeback

children.content: Your hopes of passing might come crashing down like so many drones in a harbour. But it's still worth a try.

image.altText: A close up image of a jar full of money, with a 'swear jar' label on the front.

items.title: The Aussie rocker who mastered the video game needle drop

children.content: Melbourne's Johnny Galvatron gave up the rockstar life to become a BAFTA-winning game developer. Now, his studio's latest release, Mixtape, re-imagines the music game as an eclectic playlist of genres and mechanics.

image.altText: a man in a checked shirt and black and white sunglasses leans against a blue VW beetle.

items.title: Growing garlic is one of life's slower lessons

children.content: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.

image.altText: Koren holds garlic bulbs in a basket, she's smiling.

avatar.alt: A woman sits in her front garden, which she replanted with native plants to attract local butterflies and insects.

items.title: Social media group calls for change while exposing inequality in sport

children.content: Not All Clubs calls out inequality in sporting organisations across the country. This weekend, the group is calling on teams to wear red armbands in memory of the women whose lives have been claimed by gender-based violence this year.

gallery.altText: Two AFL players stand in a huddle. Both players wear two red bands on their left arm.

thumbnail.altText: Two AFL players stand in a huddle. Both players wear two red bands on their left arm.

items.title: Saints' tough loss made worse as midfielder injures Achilles

children.content: Hawthorn consolidates its top four spot with a big win over St Kilda, as the Saints lose Sam Flanders to a potentially serious Achilles injury.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFL player is supported by two medical staff as he makes his way off the ground.

items.title: Simpson shows no interest in Bombers job while Hird has 'inside running'

children.content: Despite being Brad Scott's immediate replacement, Essendon interim coach Dean Solomon is not throwing up his hands for the full-time role any time soon.

image.altText: A mid-shot of West Coast Eagles coach Adam Simpson on ground with a blue and yellow team jumper on, looking upwards.

items.title: Insect repellent may not always keep mozzies away, study suggests

children.content: Scientists have found that mosquitoes in the lab can be trained to connect the smell of a widely used repellent with food, but repellents are still the best way to protect yourself.

image.altText: Close up photo of a mosquito on a green leaf.

items.title: Council buys land for $1, but what's the catch?

children.content: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

image.altText: A drone image of a block of land with bushland on surrounded by buildings, road and the beach.

items.title: Rodent faeces, animal blood, mould spark $60k penalty for country bakery

children.content: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread and rodent faeces on food at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

gallery.altText: A wide of a shop front

thumbnail.altText: A wide of a shop front
Close of three awards behind a window
